title: 10 best herbal creams for arthritis

remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric's active compounds, including curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.

Cyclooxygenase inhibition: Turmeric's ability to inhibit the production of cyclooxygenase enzymes helps reduce the body's production of inflammatory prostaglandins, leading to decreased joint swelling and pain in individuals with arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of arthritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound, curcumin, has been shown to reduce joint inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, curcuma longa creams may improve joint mobility and reduce stiffness by promoting the repair of damaged tissues. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of curcuma longa creams make them a promising alternative to conventional treatments for arthritis, providing relief and improving the quality of life for those suffering from the condition.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A phenolic compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce joint inflammation and alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Ar-turmerone (Ar-turmerone): A sesquiterpene that has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing joint pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A phenolic compound that has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as the ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to arthritis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of Curcuma longa, rhyzomes contain high levels of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also used to make creams for arthritis, as they contain similar anti-inflammatory properties as the rhyzomes.
Stems: Stems of Curcuma longa, although less commonly used, may also be used to make creams for arthritis, as they contain some of the same beneficial compounds as the rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Melt 120 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat over 30 minutes.
Mix 30 grams of turmeric powder with 60 grams of shea butter in a bowl until well combined.
Add the melted coconut oil to the turmeric and shea butter mixture and stir until smooth.
Whip the mixture for 10 minutes using a stand mixer until it becomes light and fluffy.
Pour the cream into glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols, shogaols, and paradols that help reduce inflammation in the joints, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with arthritis.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: Herbal ginger creams also contain analgesics that can help relieve pain and discomfort caused by arthritis, allowing for increased mobility and reduced reliance on prescription medications.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in herbal ginger creams can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to the development of chronic conditions like arthritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in the joints. When applied topically, these compounds can help to decrease swelling and ease stiffness, providing relief from the symptoms of arthritis. Additionally, ginger has natural antioxidant properties that can help to protect the joints from further damage, promoting overall joint health and well-being. This makes ginger creams a popular natural remedy for arthritis sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These iridoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols are also responsible for ginger's anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, providing relief from arthritis symptoms.
6-Gingerol: This specific gingerol has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the development of arthritis, thereby reducing inflammation and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are also commonly used for their bioactive compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Buds: Ginger buds, which are the young, unopened flowers, are used for their high content of volatile oils and bioactive compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh zingiber officinale roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Grate 1/2 cup of the cleaned roots using a fine grater or a microplane to obtain a smooth paste.
Infuse the grated root paste in 1 cup of distilled water for 30 minutes in a double boiler.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid extract.
Mix the extract with 1/2 cup of a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a glass bowl until well combined.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: St John's Wort cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as hyperforin and adhyperforin, can reduce swelling and pain in joints affected by arthritis.

Pain relief: The creams' active ingredients, including flavonoids and terpenes, have analgesic properties that help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with conditions like osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort cream's antioxidant properties can help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of arthritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, penetrate deep into the skin and joints, reducing inflammation and pain associated with arthritis. By blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, Hypericum perforatum creams alleviate the symptoms of arthritis, providing relief and improving joint mobility. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It helps with arthritis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, reducing pain and inflammation associated with the condition.
Quercetin: It helps with arthritis by acting as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the joints.

Naphthodianthrones (e.g., Hypericin): They help with arthritis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response to alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most used part due to their high concentration of hyperforin and hypericin, which are bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Flowers: Flowers are another commonly used part, rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phloroglucinols, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects.
Seeds: Seeds are also used, particularly for their oil content, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties when used topically in creams for arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves on a sunny day in mid-summer.
Clean and dry the herbs in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Infuse 100g of the dried herbs in 1 liter of coconut oil at 50°c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Whip the oil mixture with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter until smooth consistency is achieved.

remedy_properties: Salicin content: White willow creams contain salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the joints.
Anti-inflammatory activity: The flavonoids present in white willow cream have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and joint stiffness associated with arthritis.
Antioxidant activity: White willow cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to joints, thereby reducing the progression of arthritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salix alba, also known as white willow, creams helps with arthritis because it contains salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin. Salicin works by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which cause pain and inflammation. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of salicin help to reduce joint pain and stiffness associated with arthritis. By applying Salix alba creams directly to the affected area, it can provide quick relief from arthritis symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for managing this condition.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Salicin, a phenolic compound similar to aspirin, helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Tannins: Tannins, a type of polyphenol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce joint pain and inflammation, as well as protect against oxidative stress.

Salicortin: Salicortin, a triterpenoid saponin, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that may help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce pain associated with arthritis.
Leaves: Utilized for their astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Stems: Employed for their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making them a potential ingredient in creams for arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried salix alba bark from sustainably managed trees at dawn when moisture is low.
Steep the dried salix alba bark in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids immediately.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax and 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 5 minutes.
Mix the cooled herbal liquid with the melted beeswax and coconut oil blend and pour into jars.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's claw creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagoside, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in joints affected by arthritis.
Analgesic properties: The analgesic properties of devil's claw help to numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort associated with arthritis.

Pain-relieving glycosides: Devil's claw creams contain glycosides like harpagide, which have been shown to have potent pain-relieving effects by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to inflammation and pain in arthritic joints.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Harpagophytum procumbens, also known as devil's claw, creams helps with arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The cream is derived from the roots of the Harpagophytum plant, which has been traditionally used to alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with arthritis. The active compounds, such as harpagoside, work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, thereby reducing swelling and discomfort in the joints. This natural approach can provide relief and improve mobility for individuals suffering from arthritis. Regular application of the cream can lead to improved joint health.
remedy_constituents: Harpagoside: This iridoid glycoside is a key bioactive compound in Harpagophytum procumbens, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid): Phenolic acids present in Devil's Claw have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in joints associated with arthritis.

Iridoid glucosides (e.g., procumbides): These iridoid glucosides have been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in patients with arthritis by modulating the body's pain response.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of Harpagophytum procumbens' active compounds, which are used to reduce inflammation and alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, rhyzomes contain the same active compounds, making them a suitable alternative or complementary part for use in creams.
Stems: Although less commonly used than roots and rhyzomes, stems still contain some of the plant's active compounds, which can be extracted and used in creams for arthritis relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried harpagophytum procumbens roots, cut into smaller pieces for easier infusion.
Combine the dried roots with 2 liters of 80 proof ethanol in a large glass container for 2 weeks.
Filter the liquid through a coffee filter to remove the plant material, then discard the solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Combine the filtered liquid with the melted wax mixture in a stainless steel bowl and stir until well combined.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal bell pepper creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like capsicum and cucurbitacin E, which help reduce inflammation and pain in joints associated with arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: Bell peppers are rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic diseases like arthritis.

Pain-relieving compounds: Herbal bell pepper creams contain pain-relieving compounds like capsaicin, which binds to receptors in the skin and nerves, blocking the transmission of pain signals and providing relief from joint pain associated with arthritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The capsaicin present in Capsicum annuum creams has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in the joints. By blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, capsaicin reduces the sensation of pain and alleviates inflammation. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to relieve arthritis symptoms, providing relief from joint pain and stiffness without the need for pharmaceuticals. It promotes a healthy inflammatory response, improving joint mobility and function.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin, a phenolic compound, helps with arthritis by inhibiting the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing pain and inflammation.
Dihydrocapsaicin (DHC): DHC, a phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce joint inflammation and pain associated with arthritis.

Catechins: Catechins, a type of flavonoid, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the joints, thereby alleviating arthritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are rich in capsaicin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate arthritis pain.
Seeds: They contain capsaicin and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: They are a good source of capsaicin and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate arthritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 ripe capsicum annuum fruits in the morning after the dew has evaporated for freshness.
Dry the harvested capsicum annuum fruits in a single layer at 35-40°c for 7-10 days to remove moisture.
Grind the dried capsicum annuum into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2-3 minutes.
Combine 250ml of distilled water with 20g of the capsicum annuum powder and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 20-25 minutes or until the beeswax has fully dissolved and a smooth cream forms.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wormwood creams contain flavonoids, lignans, and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling, pain, and stiffness in joints associated with arthritis.

Pain-relieving agents: The terpene content in wormwood, including sesquiterpenes like artemorin, has been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from arthritis-related pain.
Antioxidant activity: Wormwood's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to joint damage and exacerbate symptoms of arthritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium, also known as wormwood, creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds present in wormwood, such as thujone and sesquiterpene lactones, have been found to reduce inflammation and pain associated with arthritis. These compounds work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which contribute to joint inflammation and pain. The creams allow for localized application, targeting the affected areas and providing relief from arthritis symptoms. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and swelling in arthritic joints.
Bornyl acetate: A monoterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate arthritis symptoms by reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress.

Cynaropicrin: A sesquiterpene lactone that has potent anti-inflammatory and antinociceptive (pain-relieving) properties, contributing to the overall analgesic and anti-arthritic effects of Artemisia absinthium creams.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Artemisia absinthium leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of thujone and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

Stems: The stems of Artemisia absinthium contain a high amount of sesquiterpenes, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them a popular choice for arthritis creams.

Flowers: Artemisia absinthium flowers are used due to their content of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm arthritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Infuse 50g of dried artemisia absinthium in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids.
Mix 100g of the strained oil with 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: English lavender contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool, linalyl acetate, and camphor, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in English lavender cream, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to joint degeneration and arthritis.

Analgesic and anxiolytic properties: Lavender's analgesic and anxiolytic compounds, like linalool, have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress-related pain, making it easier for people with arthritis to manage their symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, in English lavender creams have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis. The creams also have a soothing effect on the skin, reducing redness and swelling. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate joint pain and discomfort, providing relief and improving the overall quality of life for individuals suffering from arthritis. Regular use has also been known to promote relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps reduce inflammation and pain in arthritis by modulating the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate arthritis symptoms by blocking pain signals and reducing inflammation.

Caffeic acid: A phenolic compound that has been demonstrated to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect joints from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool, which help soothe and calm arthritis symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils like linalool and linalyl acetate, they have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Stems: They contain a higher concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in reducing arthritis symptoms and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers from mature plants in the early morning.
Combine 1/2 cup of distilled water with 2 tablespoons of dried flowers in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes over low heat then strain the liquid through cheesecloth.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Add 1/4 cup of the infused liquid to the wax mixture and blend until smooth.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Eucalyptol and other terpenoids in Tasmanian Blue Gum cream have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with arthritis.
Pain-relieving properties: The oil extracted from Tasmanian Blue Gum leaves contains camphor, a natural analgesic that can help ease joint pain and stiffness caused by arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Tasmanian Blue Gum cream may help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to the development and progression of arthritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active compound, eucalyptol, penetrates deep into the skin, reducing pain and swelling associated with arthritis. It also helps to relax muscles and improve circulation, providing relief from stiffness and discomfort. Additionally, the cooling sensation of the cream provides instant relief, making it a popular choice among individuals seeking natural remedies for managing arthritis symptoms. Regular application can lead to improved mobility and reduced pain.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and swelling associated with arthritis.
Caffeic Acid: This phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce joint inflammation and damage caused by arthritis.
α-Terpineol: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, and can help to reduce pain and stiffness associated with arthritis, while also improving joint mobility.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Barks: Utilized for their rich content of tannins and flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Essential Oil from Leaves: This is used for the same reasons as leaves but the oil is often more concentrated and can penetrate deeper into the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature plants in a shaded area for 3 hours.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 40 degrees celsius for 30 minutes while occasionally stirring the contents.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store it in a cool dark place for 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Cayenne creams contain capsaicin, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and pain associated with arthritis.
Pain relief: Capsaicin in cayenne cream binds to nerve endings, blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing temporary relief from chronic joint pain.
Vasodilation: Cayenne's active compounds help dilate blood vessels, increasing circulation and reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate stiffness and soreness associated with arthritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum frutescens, also known as cayenne, creams helps with arthritis because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsaicin in cayenne peppers blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. As a result, the pain associated with arthritis is reduced, providing relief to those suffering from joint pain and inflammation. The topical application of the cream allows the capsaicin to target the affected area, providing localized relief from arthritis symptoms. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate arthritis pain.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicinoids: Capsaicinoids, particularly capsaicin, help with arthritis by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): Flavonoids in Capsicum frutescens have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with arthritis.
Polysaccharides (Arabinogalactan): Polysaccharides, such as arabinogalactan, may stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, potentially providing relief from arthritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits are used to make creams for arthritis due to their high capsaicin content, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make creams for arthritis due to their ability to provide a sustained release of capsaicinoids, which can provide long-lasting pain relief.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams for arthritis due to their rich content of capsaicinoids, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh capsicum frutescens leaves and flowers from a pesticide-free environment on a sunny day.
Combine the harvested plant material with 500ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture in a warm dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then transfer the infused oil to a clean container.
Mix 50g of the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating gently until the mixture is smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with arthritis because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce joint pain and swelling. These natural compounds also improve mobility by reducing stiffness and inflammation in the joints. Additionally, frankincense has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, further alleviating arthritic symptoms. By incorporating frankincense creams into a daily routine, individuals with arthritis may experience significant relief from discomfort and improved joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant: Frankincense cream has antioxidant properties that help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing joint degeneration and improving overall joint health.
Analgesic: The boswellic acids in frankincense also have analgesic (pain-relieving) effects, providing relief from chronic pain associated with arthritis without causing habituation or addiction.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Frankincense rhyzomes contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing joint pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Barks: The barks of frankincense contain a compound called oleo-gum-resin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to soothe and calm arthritic joints.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried boswellia serrata resin and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the powdered boswellia resin with 100 grams of beeswax and 200 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add 20 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of frankincense essential oil to the mixture, stirring well to combine.
Remove from heat, let cool, and pour into sterilized containers, allowing the cream to set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with arthritis because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. The antioxidants and essential oils present in sage have been shown to ease stiffness and improve joint mobility, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the symptoms of osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. By applying topical sage cream directly to affected joints, individuals can experience a reduction in pain and discomfort, allowing them to enjoy improved mobility and flexibility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain associated with arthritis.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage joints and exacerbate arthritis symptoms, promoting overall joint health and reducing oxidative stress.

Pain-relieving flavonoids: The flavonoids present in sage, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from arthritis-related pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the sage plant are rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal creams for arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in the morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for two days and then store in airtight containers.
Combine 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis with 40 grams of coconut oil and heat the mixture at 60°c for one hour.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and let it cool to room temperature.
Add 2 teaspoons of beeswax pellets to the cooled oil mixture and melt in a double boiler heating gently until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce joint pain and swelling. The antioxidants present in rosemary also help to neutralize free radicals, preventing further damage to joints. Additionally, rosemary has been shown to increase blood circulation, allowing for better nutrient delivery and waste removal from the affected areas. This natural combination of benefits makes rosemary creams a popular alternative treatment for alleviating arthritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary cream's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in joints, which can alleviate arthritis symptoms like pain and stiffness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, can help reduce joint swelling and pain by blocking the production of inflammatory enzymes and chemicals.

Pain-relieving properties: Rosemary cream's analgesic compounds, including cineole and bornyl acetate, can help numb the area and relieve chronic pain associated with arthritis, allowing for improved mobility and function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary roots are used to reduce joint pain and inflammation.
Barks: Containing phenolic acids and flavonoids, rosemary barks help to relieve arthritic pain and stiffness by reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning when their essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or air circulation methods within 24 hours of collection for optimal preservation.
Combine 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain and filter the infused oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter, then mix with emulsifiers and preservatives as needed before packaging.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with arthritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint oil relaxes tense muscles, reduces swelling, and relieves joint pain and stiffness. Additionally, peppermint's analgesic properties numb the affected area, providing quick relief from chronic pain associated with osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and fibromyalgia. By reducing inflammation and easing discomfort, herbal peppermint creams provide a natural solution for managing arthritic symptoms, allowing individuals to regain mobility and flexibility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, which help reduce inflammation and relieve joint pain and stiffness associated with arthritis.

Pain-relieving glycosides: The glycosides present in peppermint creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to have potent analgesic properties, providing relief from chronic pain and discomfort caused by arthritis.

Antioxidant properties: Peppermint creams' antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and cell damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with arthritis.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant have been traditionally used to make salves and ointments for relieving joint pain and stiffness, as they are rich in tannins and volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase organic dried peppermint.
Combine one cup of dried peppermint leaves with two cups of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Steep the peppermint mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes then strain and discard solids.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil at a ratio of three parts oil to one part wax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream reaches a consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with arthritis because they contain rosmarinic acid, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets specific enzymes responsible for joint pain and stiffness. This natural ingredient has been shown to reduce swelling and inflammation in joints, providing relief from arthritis symptoms such as pain, stiffness, and limited mobility. Additionally, lemon balm's calming properties can help soothe irritated skin and promote relaxation, further alleviating discomfort associated with arthritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lemon balm creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in lemon balm cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage joints and surrounding tissues, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by arthritis.

Pain relief and relaxation: Lemon balm's essential oils, including citral and nerol, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps reduce muscle spasms and tension associated with arthritis pain, leading to improved sleep quality and reduced morning stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps reduce joint pain and swelling.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the lemon balm plant are rich in essential oils that help soothe arthritis-related inflammation and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves from a pesticide-free area and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Dry the leaves in a cool dark place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Combine 250 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat for 2-3 hours to create a infused oil preparation.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and mix it with beeswax and vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain in joints. The cream's active compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, penetrate deep into the skin to target affected areas. This natural remedy also contains antioxidants that help combat free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with arthritis. As a result, pot marigold creams provide relief from joint stiffness, redness, and discomfort, making it an effective and natural solution for managing arthritic symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce joint swelling and pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties in pot marigold cream work to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to joints, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that contributes to arthritis development.
Emollient: The emollient properties of pot marigold cream help soothe and lubricate dry, inflamed skin and joints, providing relief from stiffness and discomfort often experienced with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis.
Seeds: Containing flavonoids and other phytochemicals, seeds are known for their analgesic and antispasmodic effects, making them effective in relieving arthritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at peak bloom and dry them thoroughly in a warm dark place for 7-10 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried calendula flowers with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 4 hours to allow for optimal extraction of flavonoids and terpenes.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth and discard the solids before mixing it with an emulsifier like beeswax and vitamin e oil.
Pour the resulting cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with arthritis because they provide a soothing and anti-inflammatory relief to joints affected by osteoarthritis. The herbal extracts in marshmallow creams, such as white willow bark and ginger, work together to reduce joint pain and swelling. Additionally, the creamy texture of marshmallow creams can help to lubricate dry, stiff joints, making movement easier and more comfortable. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for individuals who experience chronic arthritis pain and stiffness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like mucilages, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with arthritis by coating and protecting inflamed joints.

Emollient and soothing properties: The cream's emollient and soothing properties help to calm irritated skin and joints, reducing redness and swelling, and promoting relaxation and comfort in individuals with arthritis.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow herbal creams contain antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to joint pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and lubricate joints affected by arthritis, reducing inflammation and pain.
Buds: Contain high amounts of flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing joint pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried marshmallow root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground marshmallow root with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a small saucepan and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any solids and discard the solids.
Mix the resulting liquid with 200 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature before storing it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with arthritis because of its unique ability to soothe and protect joint tissue. The mucilage extracted from slippery elm bark forms a protective barrier on the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with arthritis. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected joints. By providing a gentle, non-irritating barrier, slippery elm creams can provide long-lasting relief for those suffering from arthritis, allowing them to move more comfortably and maintain their daily activities.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Slippery elm cream helps to create a protective barrier on the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by arthritis, allowing for faster healing and pain relief.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in slippery elm, such as mucilages and phenolic acids, help to reduce swelling and pain associated with arthritis, making it an effective natural remedy for joint pain and stiffness.

Soother and moisturizer property: Slippery elm's soothing and moisturizing properties help to hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and irritation that can exacerbate arthritis symptoms, while also providing long-lasting relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and moisturize skin irritated by arthritis, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Barks: The barks contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis, making them effective in topical creams for pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry 10-15 grams of fresh ulmus rubra bark in a well-ventilated area for at least two weeks.
Combine the dried ulmus rubra bark with 30ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to create a decocotion.
Strain the decocotion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving 10ml of the infused oil.
Mix 2% beeswax with 98% of the reserved infused oil in a double boiler and heat until the wax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 180-190°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with arthritis because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and relax muscles and joints. The cream's active ingredients, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work to reduce swelling and pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation. This natural anti-inflammatory response helps to alleviate joint stiffness, pain, and discomfort associated with arthritis, allowing for improved mobility and reduced disability.
remedy_properties: Analgesic properties: Valerian contains compounds that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with arthritis, providing relief from joint inflammation and stiffness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the joints, reducing pain and stiffness and improving joint mobility.
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian's muscle-relaxing effects can help ease spasms and cramps associated with arthritis, promoting relaxation and reducing tension in the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in compounds like falcarinol and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhizomes contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh valeriana officinalis roots to ensure maximum potency and effectiveness of the cream.
Combine one part dried valeriana officinalis root powder with nine parts carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for approximately 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ingredients are fully incorporated and the mixture is warm but not hot.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any solids and ensure a smooth texture, allowing it to cool completely before use.
Transfer the cooled cream to an air-tight container and store in the refrigerator for up to two weeks, using as needed to soothe arthritis symptoms.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for arthritis are topical creams that combine natural ingredients to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with arthritis. They help by reducing joint inflammation and relieving pain, improving mobility and overall quality of life. The best herbal creams for arthritis are made from Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Capsicum annuum, Aloe barbadensis, Arnica montana, Equisetum arvense, Camellia sinensis, Ruscus aculeatus, and Salix alba. If left untreated, arthritis can lead to debilitating pain, limited mobility, and a reduced quality of life, causing individuals to experience chronic fatigue, decreased productivity, and strained relationships.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to arthritis that are treated with herbal creams are fibromyalgia, tendinitis, and bursitis. These conditions cause chronic pain, inflammation, and stiffness in the joints and muscles. Herbal creams containing natural anti-inflammatory agents like capsaicin, arnica, and menthol can provide relief from these symptoms by reducing pain and swelling, promoting relaxation, and improving circulation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with arthritis is turmeric and ginger-based creams. Turmeric contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces joint pain and swelling. Ginger, on the other hand, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm inflamed joints. When combined, these two herbs create a powerful and natural remedy for arthritis. Other herbs like arnica and lavender can also be added to enhance the cream's effectiveness and promote relaxation. Regular application can provide long-lasting relief from arthritis symptoms.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytotherapy Research, the use of herbal creams, such as topical capsaicin, avocado-soybean unsaponifiables, and the Chinese herbal mixture SKI306X, has shown benefit in the alleviation of osteoarthritic pain in 35 studies, with 22 different herbal medicinal products (HMPs) being evaluated. These HMPs were compared to inert or active controls in patients with osteoarthritis, and no serious side effects were reported with any of the herbal interventions. Overall, the study suggests that herbal creams may be a viable alternative to synthetic medications, reducing reliance on these medications and associated risks of adverse events.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from the active compound curcumin. This compound has been shown to reduce joint pain and inflammation by blocking the production of inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, curcumin has antioxidant properties that help protect the joints from oxidative stress, which can exacerbate arthritis symptoms. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Curcuma longa creams can provide relief from arthritis symptoms, improving joint mobility and overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Acts as a potent anti-inflammatory agent, reducing joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Exhibits strong antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative damage that can contribute to arthritis symptoms.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): Inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, such as prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which are involved in the inflammation and pain associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate arthritis symptoms.

Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds, making them a popular choice for arthritis creams due to their potential anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, the seeds of Curcuma longa may be used in some arthritis creams due to their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Melt 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder and 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the melted oil.
Stir well and heat for another 5 minutes until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 40°c for 10 minutes.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it becomes light and fluffy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the active compound gingerol. This natural ingredient reduces joint pain and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The warming sensation produced by ginger also increases blood flow to the affected areas, promoting healing and relaxation. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress, a key factor in arthritis progression. As a result, Zingiber officinale creams provide a natural, effective relief from arthritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help alleviate arthritis symptoms by reducing inflammation and pain through their potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Shogaols: Act as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, reducing joint inflammation and pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Paradol: Exert its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the joints, thus providing relief from arthritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of ginger due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, which provide relief from arthritis pain.
Roots: Ginger roots are also used due to their rich content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate arthritis symptoms.

Barks: Ginger barks are occasionally used, although less frequently than rhyzomes or roots, due to their lower concentration of active compounds, but they still contain some anti-inflammatory properties that can help with arthritis relief.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 50g of dried zingiber officinale in 500ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Combine 50g of beeswax and 10g of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Add 100ml of the infused oil to the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean tin and allow it to set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with arthritis because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in joints. The creams also have antioxidant and soothing effects that can calm irritated tissues and promote relaxation. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra's flavonoids and glycosides may help to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating arthritis symptoms. This natural remedy can provide relief and comfort for those suffering from arthritis, promoting a smoother and more manageable daily life.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: This anti-inflammatory compound helps with arthritis by reducing inflammation and suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Glycyrrhizin: This saponin has anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, which help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis such as pain and swelling.
Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce the severity of arthritis symptoms and slow down joint damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Leaves: Used due to their antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in arthritic joints.
Barks: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help reduce pain and swelling in arthritic joints.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reliable supplier.
Grind the glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the glycyrrhiza glabra powder with 100g of distilled water and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally with a spatula.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and transfer the resulting cream into a clean glass jar.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum creams helps with arthritis because they contain capsaicin, a natural compound that blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. This leads to a decrease in pain perception and inflammation reduction. Capsaicin also has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and stiffness in the joints, making it easier to move and perform daily activities. Regular use of these creams can provide long-term relief from arthritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Helps reduce arthritis pain by blocking the production and release of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Capsaicinoid glycosides: Contributes to the anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of Capsicum annuum by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
Flavonoids: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate arthritis symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and scavenging free radicals.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The most commonly used part is the fruit of Capsicum annuum, which is rich in capsaicin, a compound that helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum also contain capsaicin and other beneficial compounds that can help to relieve arthritis symptoms, making them a popular choice for topical creams.
Seeds: The seeds of Capsicum annuum contain a higher concentration of capsaicin than the fruit, making them a valuable ingredient in creams designed to target arthritis pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of dried capsicum annuum in 500ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids within 30 minutes.
Combine 200g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter in a double boiler within 10 minutes.
Add the infused oil to the melted wax mixture and heat for 5 minutes stirring constantly.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and set within 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, are known to reduce joint inflammation and pain. It also promotes tissue repair and collagen synthesis, which can help to rebuild damaged joint tissues. Additionally, aloe vera's natural cooling effect can provide relief from joint pain and stiffness, making it a popular natural remedy for arthritis sufferers. Regular use may also improve joint mobility and function.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with arthritis.
Glycoproteins: These molecules have been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's immune response and reduce inflammation in arthritic joints.
Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the joints from damage and reduce pain and stiffness associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a gel-like substance with anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to relieve arthritis symptoms.
Gel from Leaves: It's a rich source of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Aloe Vera Juice from Leaves: It has anti-arthritic properties that help to reduce joint pain, swelling, and stiffness associated with arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of aloe barbadensis gel and transfer it to a blender for mixing.
Combine 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler for melting.
Add 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of jojoba oil to the melted mixture for blending.
Mix 1 gram of sodium citrate and 1 gram of potassium sorbate with 10 grams of distilled water for preservative solution.
Gradually add the preservative solution to the blended mixture and mix for 5 minutes for finalizing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the joints. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, work to soothe and calm inflamed tissues, promoting relaxation and reducing stiffness. By penetrating deep into the skin, Arnica montana cream provides localized relief, allowing patients to move more comfortably and maintain their range of motion. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative for those seeking a non-pharmacological approach to managing arthritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Triterpene saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which help reduce the inflammation and pain associated with arthritis.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Arnica montana have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the joints, alleviating arthritis symptoms.

Acidic triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, contributing to the relief of arthritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Utilized for their ability to provide a rich source of flavonoids, contributing to the overall anti-inflammatory effect of the creams.
Leaves: Employed for their content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which help in reducing inflammation and alleviating arthritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried arnica montana flowers that have been dried for at least 2 weeks.
Mix 50g of the dried arnica montana flowers with 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 2 hours and 15 minutes to create an infused oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the dried arnica montana flowers.
Combine 100g of shea butter with 50g of the infused arnica montana oil and 10 drops of vitamin e oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce joint pain and swelling. The herb, also known as horsetail, contains flavonoids and glycosides that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating arthritis symptoms. Additionally, it has antioxidant and analgesic properties that help to soothe and calm the affected joints, promoting relaxation and reducing stiffness. This natural remedy can provide relief for people suffering from osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and other joint-related conditions.
remedy_constituents: Silica: Helps to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory agent.
Caffeic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing pain and swelling associated with arthritis.
Furanosesquiterpenes: Shows anti-inflammatory activity by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating arthritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their reported anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Stems: Employed for their mucilage content, which may help soothe and calm joint pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh equisetum arvense by cutting it at the base with scissors.
Dry the harvested equisetum arvense in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Combine 10 grams of dried equisetum arvense with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to 1 hour, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 60°c.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis creams helps with arthritis because they harness the anti-inflammatory properties of green tea. The antioxidants present in Camellia sinensis, also known as green tea, help to reduce joint inflammation and alleviate pain associated with arthritis. The cream's soothing and moisturizing properties also help to reduce stiffness and promote relaxation, making it easier to manage symptoms and improve overall joint health. This natural remedy has been shown to provide relief for those suffering from arthritis.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the joints, alleviating arthritis symptoms.
Theaflavins: Inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis.
Gallocatechin: Act as an antioxidant, reducing the damage caused by free radicals and thereby alleviating arthritis symptoms such as pain and stiffness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds like theaflavins and thearubigins, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with arthritis.
Stems: The stems are utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with arthritis.
Barks: The barks of Camellia sinensis are used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help relieve pain and reduce inflammation in joints affected by arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh camellia sinensis leaves at dawn when they are still slightly dewy and aromatic.
Steam 100g of fresh camellia sinensis leaves for 20 minutes to release their active compounds and oils.
Infuse 200g of dried camellia sinensis leaves in 1 liter of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Combine the infused camellia sinensis liquid with 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil to create a base mixture.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes and then pour it into 10 small glass containers to set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus creams helps with arthritis because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in joints. The creams may also improve blood circulation to the affected areas, promoting healing and relaxation. Additionally, the natural ingredients in Ruscus aculeatus creams may help reduce stiffness and improve range of motion, allowing individuals with arthritis to move more easily and comfortably. This can lead to improved overall quality of life for those suffering from arthritis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.

Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help alleviate the symptoms of arthritis, such as pain and swelling, by reducing inflammation in the affected joints.
Terpenoids: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may contribute to the relief of arthritis symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the joints.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their extracts are used to make creams to soothe and calm the skin.
Roots: Their extracts are used to make creams to reduce inflammation and promote skin health.
Stems: Their extracts are used to make creams to improve skin elasticity and firmness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh ruscus aculeatus leaves and 50g of its stems during their peak potency season.
Steam distill the harvested plant material to obtain its essential oil within 2 hours.
Combine 20g of the essential oil with 80g of a carrier oil like sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:4.
Mix the plant-based oil blend with 50g of a moisturizing base like beeswax and shea butter within 30 minutes.
Filter the herbal cream through a cheesecloth and store it in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salix alba creams helps with arthritis because they contain salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that is similar to aspirin. The salicylic acid in these creams can help reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the joints, making it easier for people with arthritis to move and perform daily activities. The anti-inflammatory properties of Salix alba creams also help to reduce swelling and stiffness in the joints, providing long-lasting relief from arthritis symptoms. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate joint pain and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: A natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol): A class of antioxidants with anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint swelling and alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Tannins: A type of polyphenol that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in arthritic joints.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of Salix alba contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce pain and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of Salix alba are rich in salicin and other anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing joint pain and inflammation.
Buds: The buds of Salix alba contain salicin and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve joint pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 part salix alba bark in 3 parts cold water for 2 hours to create a strong tea.
Strain the infusion to obtain a clear liquid and discard the solids after 30 minutes of resting time.
Combine 1/3 cup of beeswax with 1/3 cup of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl to melt.
Add 2 tablespoons of the salix alba tea to the melted wax and oil mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small jars and allow it to cool and set for 30 minutes to an hour before use.
